   namespace batch_insert_products {

   struct Product {
     sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
     std::string name;
     float price;
     std::string category;
     int stock;
   };

   bool batch_insert_products(const std::vector<Product>& products) {
     // Connect to the database
     auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect();

     // Drop the table if exists
     auto drop_op = sqlgen::drop<Product>;
     auto drop_result = drop_op(conn);
     if (!drop_result) {
       return false;
     }

     // Create the table
     auto create_op = sqlgen::create_table<Product>;
     auto create_result = create_op(conn);
     if (!create_result) {
       return false;
     }

     // Begin transaction
     auto begin_op = sqlgen::begin_transaction();
     auto begin_result = begin_op(conn);
     if (!begin_result) {
       return false;
     }

     // Insert the products
     auto insert_op = sqlgen::insert(products);
     auto insert_result = insert_op(conn);
     if (!insert_result) {
       return false;
     }

     // Commit the transaction
     auto commit_op = sqlgen::commit();
     auto commit_result = commit_op(conn);
     if (!commit_result) {
       return false;
     }

     return true;
   }

   }  // namespace batch_insert_products

 - We select e1.name as employee and e2.name as manager (which can be null).

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_product_sales_report {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string category;
};

struct Sale {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct CategoryReport {
  std::string category;
  int total_qty;
  double total_amt;
};

struct ProductSalesReport {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ProductSalesReport";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string category;
  int total_qty;
  double total_amt;
};

std::vector<CategoryReport> query_product_sales_report() {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product>("p")
                 | sqlgen::as<"category">(sqlgen::col<"category", "p">)
                 | sqlgen::sum(sqlgen::col<"quantity", "s">).as<"total_qty">()
                 | sqlgen::sum(sqlgen::col<"amount", "s">).as<"total_amt">()
                 .join<Sale>("s", sqlgen::col<"product_id", "p"> == sqlgen::col<"product_id", "s">)
                 .group_by(sqlgen::col<"category", "p">)
                 .having(sqlgen::sum(sqlgen::col<"amount", "s">) > 5000)
                 .to<std::vector<CategoryReport>>;
    return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace query_product_sales_report
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace update_order_status {

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string status;
  sqlgen::DateTime order_date;
  double total_amount;
};

int update_order_status() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Update pending orders older than 7 days to expired
  auto query1 = sqlgen::update<Order>
      .set("status", "expired")
      .where("status"_c == "pending" && "order_date"_c < sqlgen::sqlite::date("now", "-7 days"));
  
  // Update shipped orders to delivered (simulated)
  auto query2 = sqlgen::update<Order>
      .set("status", "delivered")
      .where("status"_c == "shipped");
  
  // Execute both queries and return total rows affected
  return conn->execute(query1).value() + conn->execute(query2).value();
}

} // namespace update_order_status
```</answer>

namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct Sale {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct SalesSummary {
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string product_name;
  int total_quantity;
  double total_revenue;
};

bool create_view_sales_summary() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  using namespace sqlgen::aggregations;

          sum("amount"_c("s")).as("total_revenue")
      )
      .group_by("product_name");

  auto create_view_stmt = create_as<SalesSummary>(query).if_not_exists();

  auto result = create_view_stmt(conn);
  return result.is_success();
}

}  // namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <sqlgen/literals.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/aggregations.hpp>

namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct Sale {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct SalesSummary {
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string product_name;
  int total_quantity;
  double total_revenue;
};

bool create_view_sales_summary() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  using namespace sqlgen::aggregations;

  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return false;
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product>("name", "product_id")
      .join<Sale, "s", sqlgen::join::inner>(
          sqlgen::where("product_id"_c == "product_id"_c("s")))
      .select(
          "name"_c.as("product_name"),
          sum("quantity"_c("s")).as("total_quantity"),
          sum("amount"_c("s")).as("total_revenue")
      )
      .group_by("product_name");

  auto create_view_stmt = sqlgen::create_as<SalesSummary>(query).if_not_exists();

  auto result = create_view_stmt(conn);
  return result.is_success();
}

}  // namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary
```</answer>

  // Check if any input items were not found in the database

  }

  return true;
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
  std::string location;
};

/**
 * description: inventory_bulk_write_and_read.
 * This function demonstrates how to perform bulk write and read operations on an inventory table.
 * It first drops and recreates the table, then writes the given items to the table.
 * Finally, it reads back the items with quantity > 50, orders them by quantity in descending order,
 * and returns true if at least one item is found.
 * Returns: bool.
 */
bool inventory_bulk_write_and_read(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Write the items
  sqlgen::write(conn, items);
  
  // Build the query: select from Inventory where quantity > 50, order by quantity desc
  auto query = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Inventory>> 
               | sqlgen::where("quantity"_c > 50) 
               | sqlgen::order_by("quantity"_c.desc());
  
  // Execute the query
  auto result = query(conn);
  
  // Check if any items were found
  if (!result) {
    return false;
  }
  
  return !result.value().empty();
}

}  // namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read
```</answer>
